nativeRange.habitat: Shallow tropical reefs, rocky shorelines, intertidal tide pools, and coral rubble flats; usually hides in crevices and under rock during the day and grazes at night.

decorPreferences: mature live rock with established algae film
crevices and overhangs for daytime hiding
tight-fitting lid to prevent escape from above-water excursions

diet.notes: Small cowries (Money Cowrie, Ring Cowrie) are primarily herbivorous algae grazers — film algae, diatoms, hair algae, and some cyanobacteria — and benefit from supplemental dried seaweed or algae wafers when tank algae is depleted. Large cowries (Tiger Cowrie) start as algae grazers as juveniles but mature into opportunistic omnivores that will take meaty foods (mussel, clam, silverside pieces) and have been documented nipping anemones, soft corals, and sponges. Calcium and trace-element supplementation supports shell integrity for all sizes.

summary: Cowrie Snail is an umbrella name for several Cypraeidae species sold to the reef trade as Money, Ring, and Tiger Cowries. Small cowries are generally peaceful nocturnal algae grazers, while large Tiger Cowries outgrow nano tanks and may nip corals.
careNotes: Cowrie Snail is an umbrella name covering several species in the family Cypraeidae sold to the reef trade, most commonly the Money Cowrie (Monetaria moneta, around 1 to 3 cm), the Ring Cowrie (Monetaria annulus, around 1 to 5 cm), and the much larger Tiger Cowrie (Cypraea tigris, around 10 to 15 cm). All share polished domed shells with a long toothed underside opening and a brilliant fleshy mantle that covers and uncovers the shell. The mantle is the living tissue of the animal — when extended, it sheaths the shell in patterned skin and gives cowries their characteristic glossy finish; many specimens have also been observed decorating themselves with fragments of shell, coralline rubble, or detritus. Small cowries (Money, Ring) are generally best kept in mature reef tanks of around 100 L or more with established live rock; they are nocturnal algae grazers that consume film algae, diatoms, hair algae, and some cyanobacteria, and are typically considered reef-safe. Tiger Cowries are a different husbandry proposition: they reach roughly 10 to 15 cm shell length, outgrow nano and small reef tanks, and as adults transition from algae grazing to an omnivorous diet that has been documented to include nipping anemones, sponges, and soft corals. Tiger Cowries are best treated as reef-safe with caution, housed in tanks of at least 200 L with a fish-only or large-polyp-stony-coral-tolerant (LPS) stocking plan, and are generally not recommended alongside prized soft corals or Condylactis-type anemones. All cowries are sensitive to copper and must not be kept in tanks treated with copper-based medications. Drip-acclimation over several hours is generally advised, as cowries have been reported to be sensitive to rapid changes in salinity and pH. Cowries are nocturnal and may remain hidden for days at a time, particularly when newly introduced; they have been observed briefly climbing above the waterline, so a tight-fitting lid is generally advised.
breedingNotes: Cowries are gonochoric (separate sexes) and release planktonic veliger larvae into the water column. The larvae drift in open water for an extended period and require live phytoplankton and very stable water conditions to survive; captive rearing has rarely been achieved by hobbyists and is not a practical breeding project in a typical home aquarium.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Used Money Cowrie product page (https://www.liveaquaria.com/product/8081) and Tiger Cowrie product page (https://www.liveaquaria.com/product/866) for sizing, water parameters (temp 72-79 F = 22-26 C, pH 8.1-8.4, SG 1.020-1.025, dKH 8-12), tank size minimums, reef compatibility, and the adult Tiger Cowrie's transition to nipping anemones/sponges/soft corals.

additional.name: Bulk Reef Supply
additional.notes: Designated secondary source did not return a product page for any cowrie species; BRS does not carry Cypraeidae as livestock. Swapped to Reeflings as the working secondary.

additional.name: Reeflings - Money Cowrie
additional.notes: Cross-checked Money Cowrie sizing (3 cm), temperature (24-28 C), pH (8.0-8.4), specific gravity (1.023-1.026), and reef-safe herbivore status; confirms Indo-Pacific range.

additional.name: Reeflings - Tiger Cowrie
additional.notes: Cross-checked Tiger Cowrie size (12-15 cm), 100 L minimum, depth range to 40 m, occasional soft-coral nipping caveat, and lifespan ('several years').

additional.name: Wikipedia - Monetaria moneta and Monetaria annulus
additional.notes: Confirmed taxonomy (Cypraeidae, order Littorinimorpha), Indo-Pacific distribution, intertidal/shallow-reef habitat, and shell-size ranges for the Money (~3 cm) and Ring (~0.9-5 cm) Cowries.
